utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey george how are you today i understand you're here for some numbness and tingling in your fingers and some pain in your wrist

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right my right wrist and hand has been bothering me probably for a few months now with pain and numbness

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and you said that's been ongoing for several months do you know what caused this type of pain or is it just something that started slowly or

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it just kinda started on it's own it i notice it mostly at night

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sometimes it will i'll wake up and my hands asleep and i got ta shake it out

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: shake it out and okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then some

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what kind of work do you do

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do yard work

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yard work

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: landscaping landscaping

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: landscaping okay so a lot of raking a lot of digging so a lot of repetitive type movements

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it's pretty heavy labor but it's yeah the same thing day in and day out

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay just a couple questions for you you did say that you have the pain at night in that and you have to you get that numbness into the hand is it in all the fingers

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it seems to happen to all my fingers but i notice it more in my thumb and pointer finger

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay and anything into that little into your fifth finger your little finger any numbness there at times no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sometimes yeah it seems like it's numb too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay what about your right hand any problems with that hand

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i do n't seem to have any problems with my right hand so far it's just mostly my left

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay good and just a couple you know do you how do you have many or do you drink often do you have you know many any alcohol consumption

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i drink usually a a beer or two on fridays and saturdays on the weekends

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and do you have any evidence of any anybody ever said that you had some rheumatoid arthritis in your hand or wrist anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no nobody say anything like that so i mean

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay good so let me go ahead and do a physical exam here real quick and you know i'm gon na quickly just listen to your heart and lungs okay that's good i'd like you to squeeze i'm gon na hold your hands here and i'd like you to squeeze both hands

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you seem a little bit weaker on that left hand is that what you've noticed

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i i i experienced some weakness in my left hand

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay do you you find that you're dropping things when you're picking it up is it to that level or

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i drop things mostly because i have a hard time feeling it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay good and so you you do have a a grip strength is less on the left and i just wan na touch your fingers here on the on the right side you can feel me touching all the fingers on the right

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i can i can say you touch me but it feels a little more weird on the thumb side than my pointer finger side

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay and i wan na turn your wrist over here and turn your hand over and i'm gon na go ahead and tap on the right wrist on the back here does that do anything when i do that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i still i feel a little jolt or a zing in my finger tips

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and then when i do that on the left side

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah same thing

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: same thing okay so you do have a bilateral positive tinel's sign so so here's here's where i'm at i think your your diagnosis is beginning to have some bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome usually we see that with repetitive actions such as the landscaping the heavy labor and you you know your your clinical exam and and history sound like it's a carpal tunnel syndrome i do want to order so where are we gon na go from here i would like to order a a study it's called an emg where it it measures some of that electrical impulses down into your fingers we will follow up with that but as far as your treatment so the treatment for carpal tunnel syndrome is really some activity modification now i know you are a landscaper is there any way that you could be work to have some lighter work during the time

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i suppose i could try to pass it off to some of my other employes and delegate

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that would be good so that's i i just want you to kinda eliminate that the active repetitive motions that you're doing all the time just for a couple weeks i'm also gon na give you a wrist splint to wear and that should help and i'd like you to take ibuprofen six hundred milligrams every six hours and then i wan na see you back here in the office in two weeks and in that two week period i think we're gon na see if there's need for any other intervention if i need to do more diagnostic testing or if there is a possibly looking at a surgical intervention to release that pressure that's on the nerves in that hand does that sound like a a good plan for you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it sounds like a good first start

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay so i i just just off off the record here what kind of what do what do you specialize in landscaping is your company do

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mostly like yard work and maintenance flower beds not really designing just up keep

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay yeah i'm looking for a landscape designer i need somebody to put in some elaborate walkways back through the backyard so yeah we can do stuff like that i mean if you have an idea what you want i think that's easy

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you know if you're looking for like some

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: backyard elasis rehab remodel that's i mean i suppose we could do we have n't done things like that in a while because we're busy enough with just the up key but it's something to explore

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay yeah i may have to keep that in mind because i do wan na do some of that so let's listen i'm gon na get my my nurse in here to discharge you do you have any other questions for me before we end this

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i think it's all clear i appreciate it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay take care and i'll look forward to see you in two weeks

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: very good appreciate your time

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 57-year-old male presenting with left wrist and hand pain.
Carpal tunnel syndrome
The patient reports left wrist and hand pain and numbness for a few months.
The doctor asks about the onset and cause of the patient's symptoms.
The patient reports symptoms started gradually and are worse at night.
The patient reports waking up with numbness and shaking his hands for relief.
The doctor asks about the patient's occupation.
The patient works in landscaping.
The doctor notes the patient performs repetitive motions at work.
The doctor asks if the numbness affects all fingers.
The patient reports numbness in all fingers, most noticeably in the thumb and index finger.
The doctor asks if there is numbness in the little finger.
The patient reports intermittent numbness in the little finger.
The doctor asks if the patient has problems with the right hand.
The patient denies right hand pain.
The doctor asks about alcohol consumption.
The patient consumes 1 to 2 beers on weekends.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of rheumatoid arthritis.
The patient denies a history of rheumatoid arthritis.
The doctor performs a physical exam including heart and lung auscultation and grip strength testing.
The doctor notes decreased grip strength in the left hand.
The patient confirms weakness in the left hand.
The doctor asks if the patient drops objects.
The patient reports dropping objects due to decreased sensation.
The doctor confirms decreased grip strength on the left and tests sensation on the right.
The patient reports altered sensation in the right thumb and index finger.
The doctor performs a Tinel sign test on the right wrist.
The patient reports a positive Tinel sign in the right fingertips.
The doctor performs a Tinel sign test on the left wrist.
The patient reports a positive Tinel sign in the left fingertips.
The doctor confirms a bilateral positive Tinel sign and diagnoses bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome.
The doctor orders an EMG of the bilateral upper extremities.
The doctor asks if the patient can modify his work activities.
The patient agrees to delegate repetitive tasks to employees.
The doctor instructs the patient to limit repetitive motions for 2 weeks, wear a wrist splint, and take ibuprofen 600 mg every 6 hours.
Ibuprofen 600 mg every 6 hours.
The patient will follow up in 2 weeks to review EMG results and discuss further treatment.
The doctor asks if the patient has any other questions.
The patient has no further questions.
The patient will return for a follow-up visit in 2 weeks.
